the author(s) and do not necessarily reflect the views of the U.S. Economic

4
 
February 28, 2025 
 
Greetings! 
 
As Governor of the State of Illinois, I am pleased to welcome everyone gathered for the 20th 
America’s Competitiveness Exchange (ACE) organized by the Organization of American States 
(OAS), the U.S. Department of Commerce, and the U.S. State Department, along with the State of 
Illinois, the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity (DCEO), and the 
University of Illinois System. We are honored to host this year’s event and certain that your 
experience over the next several days will foster fresh ideas and opportunities for collaborations. 
 
As the 18th largest economy in the world, Illinois offers unparalleled access to global markets with 
its central location and infrastructure, plus natural advantages such as abundant fresh water and 
fertile soil. With thriving innovation, a promising economic landscape, and a world-class 
workforce, Illinois is the best place to live, work, and do business. 
 
On behalf of the people of Illinois, I offer my best wishes for a memorable and productive 
conference, and for continued success in the years to come. 
 
Sincerely, 
 
 
 
 
 
Governor JB Pritzker

5
Welcome from 
the ACE Committee
Dear distinguished leaders from the Americas and beyond,
We are honored to welcome you to the 20th edition of the Americas 
Competitiveness Exchange (ACE) Illinois 2025. ACE is a powerful mechanism that 
fosters cooperation and creation of new partnerships to strengthen economic 
development in the Americas.
ACE Illinois is convened by the Organization of American States (OAS) and the United 
States Government, through the Department of Commerce (Economic Development 
Administration) and the Department of State (Permanent Mission to the OAS). The 
local organizer and host is the State of Illinois, through the Illinois Department of 
Commerce and Economic Opportunity and the University of Illinois System. 
Throughout your week in Illinois, April 27 to May 2, 2025, you will have the opportunity 
engage with Illinois’ vibrant industries, including Life Science, Quantum Computing, 
Energy Production, Advanced Manufacturing, Agri-Tech, and vibrant industries, 
including Life Sciences, Transportation and Logistics. Participants will meet with 
leaders from the public sector, private sector, and academia in Chicago, Champaign, 
Urbana, Joliet, Naperville and Peoria. 
We want to emphasize the importance of nurturing the connections that you establish 
over the course of this week to ensure you make the most of your time in ACE. We are 
excited for what awaits us in Illinois and the connections that will emerge from this 
week. To facilitate meaningful and strategic engagement throughout the program, we 
have compiled this Tripbook to share key information about the ACE Illinois agenda 
and participants. We encourage you to use this resource as a compass to identify 
potential business partners for trade deals, research and development, new ventures, 
soft-landing opportunities, technical exchanges, and any other collaboration.
The ACE Committee wishes to express its most sincere gratitude to the State of 
Illinois, for the exceptional agenda they have prepared for this important exchange in 
collaboration with other key stakeholders. 
We look forward to our work together.
Sincerely,
The ACE Committee

6
ILLINOIS
OVERVIEW
The State of Illinois offers an unparalleled opportunity at the epicenter of 
North America. Illinois provides companies with a world class business 
environment, a central location with easy access for any operation, a well-
educated and experienced workforce, and an opportunity to be an integral 
part of the second most economically powerful region in the country.
 
Illinois is also one of the world’s most culturally vibrant states and has become 
a magnet for the country’s brightest minds. Businesses can benefit from low 
corporate taxes and tax-free personal property that help make Illinois one of 
the most business-friendly places in the nation.  
 
 # 1 State in the Midwest for business creation 
 # 1 Metro for new & expanding companies 
 # 1 State for sustainable development 
 # 5 Highest GDP in the nation 
 # 18 Largest economy in the world 
What Sets Illinois Apart:

7
FEATURED 
INDUSTRY CLUSTERS 
Advanced Manufacturing 
Life Sciences
Next Generation Agriculture, 
Agriculture Tech and Food 
Processing
Quantum Computing, AI, and 
Microelectronics 
Transportation, 
Distribution, and Logistics 
Energy Production and 
Manufacturing

8
UIC Innovation Center 
1871
Manufacturing x Digital (MxD)
The UIC Innovation Center is a space for interdisciplinary 
innovation and education, reflecting its dynamic mission 
through its frequent changes to meet the needs of 
projects and classes. The Innovation Center gives students 
a chance to learn through experience with continuous 
collaboration, learning, making, and remaking. Corporate 
and University partners engage with the Center to generate 
human-centered solutions focused on deep research and 
exploratory development.
1871 is a private collective of trailblazing founders, visionary 
leaders, resilient innovators, and steadfast supporters in 
tech. Immerse in our programs, resources, and community 
uniquely designed for growing a vibrant tech ecosystem.
Offering programs for early stage startups, growth & late 
stage startups, and corporate innovators, 1871 inspires, 
equips, and support extraordinary tech businesses. Since 
its launch in 2012, this has raise $3.5B in venture capital and 
created over 14,500 jobs.
MxD is where innovative manufacturers go to forge their 
futures. In partnership with the Department of Defense, 
the 22,000 square-foot future factory is used to test 
and demonstrate new technology, train workforces, and 
demonstrate the need for cybersecurity in manufacturing. 
Its 22,000-sqare-foot factory floor in this state-of-the-art 
innovation center features some of the most advanced 
manufacturing equipment in the world. MxD is also the 
National Center for Cybersecurity in Manufacturing as 
designated by the US Department of Defense.
ACE
ILLINOIS
SITES

9
University of IL Research Park 
Holonyak Micro and 
Nanotechnology Lab
Integrated Bioprocessing 
Research Laboratory
The University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign: Research Park 
is an innovation hub for technology commercialization with 
a unique environment that cultivates startups and increases 
capabilities for established companies. Located on campus, 
Research Park has more than 120 companies and growing, 
employing over 2,100 people in high-technology careers.
Featuring EnterpriseWorks, the Park’s incubator for early-
stage tech firms, the Research Park also employs more than 
800 student interns contibuting to internal corprorate R&D 
and product development programs.
HMNTL is one of the country’s largest and most 
sophisticated university facilities for conducting photonics, 
microelectronics, 
biotechnology, 
and 
nanotechnology 
research. Their 15 class 100 and 1000 cleanrooms, 46 
general purpose labs, and 2,500 square foot biosafety 
level-2 bionanotechnology complex contain all the tools 
researchers need to conduct their work.
IBRL and its sister facility, the Food Science Human Nutrition 
Pilot Processing Plant, are pilot-scale facilities designed to 
help technologies in biomanufacturing, bioprocessing, and 
food processing de-risk at a commercially-relevant scale. 
Since opening in 2018, the facilities have worked with over 
130 companies on their biomanufacturing technologies 
and successfully operate as a self-supporting model on the 
University of Illinois campus.
Matter
MATTER is a global healthcare startup incubator, community 
nexus and corporate innovation accelerator headquartered 
in Chicago. Since its inception in 2015, MATTER has supported 
over 1,000 healthcare and life sciences startups, fostering a 
community of 60,000 entrepreneurs, executives, investors, 
and experts dedicated to improving health and healthcare. 
Collaborating with dozens of hospitals, health systems, 
universities, and industry-leading companies, MATTER 
accelerates innovation, advances care, and transforms lives.

10
Center for Advanced Bioenergy 
and Bioproducts Innovation
Electrical and Computer 
Engineering Building
EnterpriseWorks
The Center for Advanced Bioenergy and Bioproducts 
Innovation (CABBI), a U.S. Department of Energy-funded 
Bioenergy Research Center, is a collaboration between the 
University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign and 20 partner 
institutions. At the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, 
CABBI is housed within the Institute for Sustainability, Energy, 
and Environment (iSEE) and the Carl R. Woese Institute for 
Genomic Biology (IGB). CABBI integrates recent advances 
in agronomics, genomics, and synthetic and computational 
biology to increase the value of energy crops — using a “plants 
as factories” approach to grow fuels and chemicals.
The Electrical and Computer Engineering department at the 
University of Illinois is world-renowned for its excellence 
in both teaching and research. The building gives students 
and faculty members access to many collaborative spaces, 
including a grand lobby filled with seating and multiple 
conference rooms. Graduate students are also sharing large, 
open workspaces, which allow for communication among 
those in different research areas.
Located in Research Park, EnterpriseWorks is a technology 
business incubator on the University of Illinois Urbana-
Champaign campus that supports and provides resources 
to deep-tech entrepreneurs and startups. The 43,000 sq 
ft business incubator specializing in early stage tech firms 
provides a multitude of assistance, including research 
commercialization, entrepreneurship support, seed funding 
opportunities, networking opportunities with potential 
partners and investors, and workshops.
National Petascale Computing 
Facility
This 
88,000-square-foot 
facility 
houses 
computing, 
networking, and data systems that benefit researchers at 
Illinois and across the country. Offers top-flight physical and 
cybersecurity, including monitoring of all exit bandwidth, 
deep packet inspection and tracking of network flows. All 
equipment in the facility must comply with cybersecurity 
and operational standards.

11
Richard J. Daley College
Ecolab
The Hatchery
Located on the southwest side of Chicago, Daley College 
serves approximately 12,000 students annually across 
our two campuses through credit, Adult Education, and 
Continuing Education programming.
The 52,000 sq. ft. Manufacturing Technology & Engineering 
Center (MTEC) trains with over 1,000 students annually. A 
Center of Excellence, MTEC was designed with insight from 
City industry partners and four-year colleges and universities 
to ensure a quality curriculum in high demands fields, such 
as advanced manufacturing.
Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, 
hygiene, and infection prevention solutions and services 
that protect people and resources. Customers in more than 
40 industries choose Ecolab’s comprehensive science-based 
solutions, data-driven insights, and world-class service to 
advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, 
and optimize water conservation and energy use.
The Hatchery Chicago is a non-profit food and beverage 
incubator dedicated to helping local entrepreneurs build 
and grow successful businesses. Through cutting edge 
commercial kitchen facilities, robust entrepreneurship 
curriculums, and partnership with generous corporate 
sponsors and foundations, The Hatchery supports local 
food and beverage entrepreneurs, cultivates local job 
opportunities, and accelerates local economic growth.
The Chicago Metropolitan 
Agency for Planning - CMAP
The CMAP is a regional government agency that works 
on behalf of the seven counties, 284 municipalities, and 
8.6 million residents of Northeastern Illinois. CMAP is now 
housed in the Old Chicago Post Office.
Through the CMAP and its Data Hub, communities access 
data snapshots on a variety of demographic, housing, 
employment, land use, freight, traffic, climate, and quality 
of life studies that inform its comprehensive planning to 
advance the regional transportation system for today and 
tomorrow.

12
CenterPoint Intermodal Center
Discovery Partners Institute
Distillery Labs
America’s largest master-planned inland port. Located 40 
miles southwest of Chicago, and is strategically positioned 
at the epicenter of the region’s transportation infrastructure. 
This massive facility stretches over 6,400 acres and is home 
to over 50 tenants. 
The campus has available warehouse and distribution center 
solutions, with rail access, interline service, and carload 
service for international and domestic intermodal capacity, 
including Free Trade Zone savings.
Led by the University of Illinois System in partnership 
with top research universities, DPI does three things: train 
people for high-demand tech jobs, conduct applied R&D, 
and business building to grow Chicago’s tech ecosystem. 
With state investment and a new innovation district in 
development, DPI has the resources to attract, develop and 
leverage the most ambitious people and companies the 
region has to offer — and keep them here.
Distillery Labs includes a makerspace, office space, 
commercial 
teaching 
kitchen, 
podcast 
studio, 
co-
working space, conference rooms, event space and a 
coffee bar. Partner organizations including the Greater 
Peoria Economic Development Corporation and Minority 
Business Development Center also are located in Distillery 
Labs, providing critical resources for entrepreneurs and 
small businesses.
This first Innovis the first completed project for the Illinois 
Innovation Network. By opening doors and minds, this 
IIN hub, is connecting people, ideas and resources to 
launch and grow new businesses in the Greater Peoria 
community.
mHUB
mHUB is an innovation center for all things HardTech. 
Through prototyping labs, industry partnerships, and a 
commitment to U.S. manufacturing, the mHUB ecosystem 
is designed to remove barriers and increase the probability 
of building a sustainable business to commercialize new 
physical products.
This leading innovation center is embedded within the 
most comprehensive manufacturing economy in the 
U.S. with 12,000+ local manufacturers that generate 
anually $10 billion and employ over 565,000 people and 
surrounded by an ecosystem of over 2,000 HardTech and 
digital startups.

13
Naper Settlement
Naper Settlement is an award-winning, outdoor history 
museum offering visitors 13 acres of educational programs, 
exhibits, guided tours, and special events. Whether 
watching the blacksmith at the forge or sitting down for a 
lesson in our one-room schoolhouse, visitors are immersed 
in history as they learn about the past and how it relates to 
the present.
Willis Tower
Located in the heart of downtown Chicago, Willis Tower is 
an iconic urban destination and state-of-the art, human-
centered workplace. Standing 1,450 feet and 110 stories tall, 
Willis Tower is one of the world’s tallest buildings and recently 
underwent a restorative street-to-sky transformation.
